rulings.Case Title: THE UNITED COUNCIL OF HUMAN SERVICES VS. REGINALD "LATE NIGHT" BARNES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication As To All Of Defendant'S Affirmative Defenses
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 12, 2019 line 4. PLAINTIFF THE UNITED COUNCIL OF HUMAN SERVICES MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication As To All Of Defendant'S Affirmative Defenses GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED as to affirmative defenses of estoppel, failure to state a cause of action, breach of warranty of quiet enjoinment, retaliation, failure to comply with the lease and/or applicable regulation - no opposition filed/not affirmative defenses. Motion is DENIED as to affirmative defenses of habitability and unclean hands as Plaintiff failed to shift its burden. Plaintiffs fails to cite any authority for the proposition that these affirmative defenses are not available under the circumstances of this case based on material breach-failure to pay rent and admission that rent has been paid during the period of claimed non-payment (compare Notice of Termination and MSJ 3:7-8) . Motion is DENIED as to the affirmative defense of waiver. Triable issue of fact regarding the waiver of breach re: multiple months of non-payment coupled with relocation offer. =(501/CFH) Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment: Hearing Required to address habitability affirmative defense. Defendant to present evidence re: habitability at the hearing.
